questions: You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. A coworker mentions they ran into your manager at the gym over the weekend. What do you make of it?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. Your apartment building recently installed a new keypad entry system. How do you feel about it?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. A recruiter from a company you've never applied to sends you a LinkedIn message. What's your reaction?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. You notice your phone's battery is draining faster than usual this week. What do you think is going on?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. A neighbor you've never spoken to waves at you for the first time. What do you think?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. Your doctor orders a follow-up blood panel after your annual checkup. What goes through your mind?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. Two friends separately cancel plans with you in the same week. What do you make of it?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. A news article mentions the city is reviewing property records in your neighborhood. What's your reaction?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. You search for a product online and see an ad for it later that day. How do you explain that?
You're a naturally trusting person who takes things at face value. Your employer announces they are rolling out a new project management system to track task progress. What do you think?
